description: Lock vernacular_literature_prose_poetry to Renaissance vernacular literary prestige from Dante onward, not generic writing or the earlier medieval troubadour node.

checks.reason: The source-backed scope is anchored to Dante's Divine Comedy, dated c. 1308-21 and written in Italian rather than Latin.
Earlier French, Provençal, Sicilian, and Tuscan vernacular traditions are lineage for Dante's and later Renaissance vernacular literature.
Petrarch and Boccaccio connect Renaissance humanism to elevated vernacular literary status without making humanism the only source of vernacular writing.
